text.title: RFC 7807 Error Handling Rules
text.summary: Concrete rules for standardizing API error responses per RFC 7807.

rule.statement: Every error response must include type (URI), title (short string), and status (integer matching HTTP status). detail, instance, traceId, and errors[] are optional but strongly recommended.
rule.rationale: RFC 7807 requires type, title, status as the minimum. Without them, clients cannot build generic error-handling logic.

rule.statement: Set Content-Type to application/problem+json, not application/json.
rule.rationale: RFC 7807 mandates the problem+json media type. Clients (e.g., Spring RestClient with ProblemDetail) detect typed handling via the suffix. Using application/json silently degrades to generic parsing.

rule.statement: The status field in the body must exactly match the HTTP response status code.
rule.rationale: RFC 7807 requires they match. Mismatches appear when re-wrapping downstream errors: the outer HTTP code is 502 while the body says 400.

rule.statement: Always populate traceId. Map from the W3C traceparent header if present; otherwise generate a UUID.
rule.rationale: Without traceId, supporting a customer requires log scraping by timestamp across all services. With traceId, one string resolves the incident.

rule.statement: For 5xx errors, detail must be a generic static string. Never interpolate exception messages, stack traces, SQL, internal paths, or PII.
rule.rationale: Raw exception messages leak implementation details, internal hostnames, and sometimes credentials. Provide traceId for internal lookup instead.

rule.statement: Use errors[] only for validation failures (4xx). Each item must have field, code (machine-readable slug), and message (human-readable).
rule.rationale: errors[] is a custom extension beyond RFC 7807. Using it for non-validation errors (401, 403, 404) over-structures simple responses and surprises clients expecting the spec shape.

rule.statement: Treat type URIs as part of the API contract. Once published, changing them is a breaking change.
rule.rationale: Clients may dispatch on the type URI to handle specific error classes. Renaming validation-error to validation silently breaks those clients.

rule.statement: Register a single framework-level exception handler. Handlers must not call response.json() with a shape that differs from ProblemDetail.
rule.rationale: Per-route error shapes re-create the inconsistency the methodology exists to solve. One handler, one shape, one content-type.
